reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome real download+run on WASM, Node cross-check, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser run (headless Chrome, WASM, fresh profile → 'Download model (~430 MB)' → click → auto-init → Fill): the default masked input 'Thank you <extra_id_0> me to your party <extra_id_1> week.' produced the sentinel-delimited fill '<extra_id_0> for sending <extra_id_1> - a great <extra_id_2>.' — genuine mT5 span-corruption output, streamed. 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Node cross-check (Xenova/mt5-small, text2text-generation, q8) confirmed real multilingual span-fill: German 'Die Hauptstadt von Deutschland ist <extra_id_0>.' → '<extra_id_0> ein kleines Stadtbild <extra_id_1>', French 'Le chat est assis sur le <extra_id_0>.' → '<extra_id_0> chat <extra_id_1>'. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify/mt5-multilingual/{desktop-light,desktop-dark,mobile-light,mobile-dark}.png
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real WASM run in the browser; honest rough fills from the raw un-fine-tuned checkpoint.

rubric.evidence: text2text-generation output is a sentinel-delimited target string: '<extra_id_0> …fill0… <extra_id_1> …fill1… <extra_id_n>' matching the span-corruption objective. Semantics correct AND multilingual: the model fills a masked span in the SAME language as the input (verified EN/DE/FR in browser+Node). The page also reconstructs the sentence by slotting each fill into its blank, and shows the sentinel token count. Verified in browser + Node.
rubric.notes: Raw span-corruption format shown honestly, not relabelled as instruction-following.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mt5-small, linked to its card + google/mt5-small), task (text2text-generation), backend (WebAssembly), and quantisation (q8). Inference runs in a module Web Worker (models/mt5-multilingual/worker.js). Conformance derives declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation + inference-off-main-thread.

Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). Headless fresh profile showed 'Download model (~430 MB)'; after the click it loaded to state=ready and ran. PASS 2 (same profile, cached): the loader auto-initialised to state=ready with the action button = 'Clear cached model' (NOT a Download button) — returning users never click Load. Verified download→ready→run AND cached auto-init in headless.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified fresh-profile download + cached auto-init with no Load button.

rubric.evidence: A role=status aria-live #status region reports 'Filling…' / 'Done on WASM.' / errors; the shared loader streams download progress and offers Retry on failure; the WASM path needs no GPU so it works offline once cached. Generation failures surface on the page ('Generation failed: …'), not only the console.
rubric.notes: Runs on WASM — no WebGPU required; degrades honestly.

rubric.evidence: Multilingual preset buttons, the masked-input textarea, max-new-tokens + beams range sliders, a sample checkbox, and the Fill button are all real, labelled, keyboard-operable; the use-case pages add language chips, a click-to-mask word picker, and multi-span presets. All exercised. The raw output, reconstructed sentence, token chips (with sentinels highlighted) and decode timeline all render from the real run.

The fills, reconstruction, token chips and decode timeline are all produced by the on-device mT5 run (verified real output above). Rough/generic fills are shown as-is — the page explicitly frames the raw un-fine-tuned checkpoint's honest limits. No canned result.

Buttons expose accessible names; the input textarea and sliders have associated labels; state announced via role=status aria-live; the decode-timeline canvas has an aria-label. No content images without alt (text/table/canvas only). Conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names derive as auto-checks.

Browser matrix: desktop (1280×800) + mobile (360×740 DPR3) both measured 0px horizontal overflow. Screenshots reviewed light+dark on both classes: at-a-glance table, preset grid, controls grid (repeat(auto-fit,minmax(170px,1fr))), token chips and reconstruction all reflow cleanly; multilingual + CJK text legible; blue links visible in dark. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/verify/mt5-multilingual/{desktop,mobile}-{light,dark}.png
rubric.notes: Agent-reviewed both device classes, both themes.

rubric.evidence: All decode inference runs in a module Web Worker with a TextStreamer; the page only renders streamed tokens + the returned result, so the interaction path stays off the main thread and INP stays low. 0 console errors during the run.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy JS off the interaction path so INP stays low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the seq2seq decode loop runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only renders streamed tokens and the final reconstruction.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: models/mt5-multilingual/worker.js runs the pipeline + TextStreamer; t2t.js renders; browser run had 0 console errors and stayed responsive

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference is entirely off the main thread in a module worker.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear, associated labels.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the masked-input <textarea>, range sliders and sample checkbox have associated <label>s; presets and Fill are real buttons.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run panel markup; conformance buttons-have-names derives as pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid grids that wrap; avoid fixed widths so there is no horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .controls-grid / .preset-grid / .stat-row use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax(...)); long fills and token chips wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: browser matrix measured 0px horizontal overflow on desktop AND mobile; screenshots reviewed

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; landmarks + heading order.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #status, labelled controls, keyboard-operable buttons, canvas aria-label, header/footer landmarks.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css.

openQuestions: If a fine-tuned mT5 checkpoint (e.g. multilingual summarization / translation) is ever ONNX-exported for transformers.js, add it as a NEW slug to show a task-specialised mT5 alongside this raw-foundation demo.

followUpGoals.goal: Add an mT5 multi-model demo pairing the language detector + mT5 fill with a downstream step (e.g. embed the reconstruction) once a fine-tuned mT5 ONNX exists.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mt5-multilingual (Xenova/mt5-small, the raw multilingual T5 pretrained on 101 languages with ONLY the span-corruption objective — distinct from FLAN-T5 (instruction-tuned), T5 (English task-prefixes) and ByT5 (byte-level)). Real in-browser WASM span-fill verified in headless Chrome: the default masked input produced a genuine sentinel-delimited reconstruction, 0 console errors, 0 net failures; Node cross-checked multilingual fills (EN/DE/FR). Fresh-profile Download (~430 MB) + cached auto-init (no Load button) both verified. Desktop+mobile measured 0px overflow with light+dark screenshots reviewed; shared auto-init loader, off-main-thread streaming worker, sentinel-token 'see inside', and full a11y semantics. Honest about the rough fills of a raw, small, un-fine-tuned checkpoint — no fine-tuned mT5 ships a browser ONNX, so we show the base.
